records.ety: [D. stom must, new wort, properly, dumb; cf. F. vin muet stum. Cf. Stammer, Stoom.]

defs: Unfermented grape juice or wine, often used to raise fermentation in dead or vapid wines; must.
quotes: Let our wines, without mixture of stum, be all fine.
And with thy stum ferment their fainting cause.

defs: Wine revived by new fermentation, reulting from the admixture of must.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stummed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stumming.]

defs: To renew, as wine, by mixing must with it and raising a new fermentation.
quotes: We stum our wines to renew their spirits.
